title: Cotula radicalis
intro: Cotula radicalis, also known as Radish Root or Bachelors Buttons, is a perennial herb of the daisy family native to Europe, western Asia, and Africa. The health benefits of Cotula radicalis include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant, making it a traditional remedy for various respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cotula radicalis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat coughs, colds, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Cotula radicalis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to Australia and New Zealand. The scientific classification of Cotula radicalis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Cotula, Species: C. radicalis. This plant is often found growing in moist, shaded areas and can be identified by its small, yellow flowers. The specific name 'radicalis' likely refers to the plant's radical or taproot.
